figures.caption: Table 1: Performance across in-domain and out-of-domain datasets for PHI-3 on MATHINSTRUCT, batch size ∣B∣ = 128 and total budget k = 64. For completeness and a fair evaluation, results are reported under two configurations for all baselines: source-wise(left of “/”) and batch-wise (right of “/”). UniPROT consistently beats baselines in both configurations.
Figure 4: Validation perplexity dynamics on PHI-3, ZEPHYR-3B and PHI-2 during training vs top 3 best-performing baselines on MATHINSTRUCT. UniPROT-PS consistently outperforms other baselines.

Figure 3: Minority Class Accuracy Analysis:On CIFAR10-LT and synthetic MNIST, UniPROT outperforms k-medoids on minority classes. (Top Left): Average minority-class accuracy vs. prototype count. (Bottom Left): CIFAR10 minority class-wise accuracy (avg. over 3 runs). (Top Right): MNIST with Induced Skew: (i) classes 0 and 5 both at 5% each (other classes at 90%), and (ii) classes 0 and 7 both at 3% each (other classes at 94%). (Bottom Right): UniPROT on average out-performs k-medoids across different skew variations for minority classes.

Figure 7: Approximate vs. actual marginal gain as a function of the entropic regularization parameter λ. Increasing λ reduces the approximation error, with the marginal gain ratio converging to 1 and exhibiting low variance for larger λ values.
Figure 1: k-medoids (4) consistently learn skewed weights for prototypes on CIFAR10. The plot shows ranked weights of prototypes, averaged over 5 runs.

Table 6: Per-batch performance across in-domain and out-of-domain datasets for PHI-3 on MATHINSTRUCT, batch size ∣B∣ = 128 and total budget k = 64. We compare FT, GradNorm, COLM, GREATS, and UniPROT (Ours).

Table 7: Top: Per-batch performance of FT, GradNorm, COLM, GREATS, and UniPROT on MATHINSTRUCT with ∣B∣ = 128, k = 64. Bottom: Validation perplexity when ∣B∣ = 128, subset ratio 50%, and prototype selection is batch-wise.
Figure 9: Zephyr-3b Validation perplexity when bs=128 and subset ratio 25% and prototype selection is batch wise.

Table 8: Performance across in-domain and out-of-domain datasets for ZEPHYR-3B on MATHINSTRUCT, batch size ∣B∣ = 128 and total budget k = 32. Results are reported under two configurations for all baselines: source-wise (left of “/”) and batch-wise (right of “/”).

Table 9: Performance across in-domain and out-of-domain datasets for PHI-2 on MATHINSTRUCT, batch size ∣B∣ = 128 and total budget k = 32. Results are reported under two configurations for all baselines: source-wise (left of “/”) and batchwise (right of “/”).
